log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, tagged as a 'spreader' with 'persistence' and 'overlay' tags. The size is 577944 bytes. I need to get all IOC types to understand the full scope of artifacts available

The IOC summary confirms 1 domain (VBOXSVR.ovh.net), 1 registry persistence key, and 45 dropped files. I need to investigate the network activity to see the full DNS resolution and any HTTP connection

Network activity shows only one DNS lookup to vboxsvr.ovh.net with no resolved IPs, suggesting the C2 or download server might be blocked or the connection failed. The main focus should be on the drop

The dropped files reveal a classic 'dropper/spreader' pattern. The malware drops numerous files with pornographic names into legitimate system directories (Program Files, Windows, etc.) to blend in an

Registry changes confirm the persistence mechanism: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\mssrv32 points to C:\Windows\mssrv.exe. This is a classic Run key persistence technique. Now I ne

Process activity shows no processes created (likely due to sandbox limitations or delayed execution), but notable modules include network-related DLLs (mswsock, ws2_32, netapi32, rpcrt4) and system DL
